$GOOGL plans to use ~$30B of its $80B equity raise to cover tax obligations tied to employee stock awards. 

That represents ~40% of the raise which is about double last year’s level and ~14% of expected operating cash flow.

$NET CEO says bots and agents are now generating more internet traffic than humans for the first time.

The agentic internet is arriving faster than expected.

You can watch my full $NVTS AI power breakdown here:

piped.video/91iawV0FTXw?si=vi0d…

2026 IS THE YEAR OF MEMORY

• $SNDK +516%
• SK Hynix +245%
• $MU +208%
• Samsung +147%

AI is becoming memory-first as models and agents need longer context, faster retrieval and more data kept closer to compute.
The market is taking a much-needed breather today.
$BMNR is now trading at its lowest level since Tom Lee came onboard last summer.

Quantinuum is seeking up to $1.46B at a $14B valuation in what could be the largest quantum IPO to date.

$META launched Business Agent across WhatsApp, Instagram and Messenger with more than 1M businesses already using the product.

Meta is also rolling out an enterprise platform with $SHOP and $SE Shopee integrations with paid plans expected in coming months.

$GOOGL upsized its capital raise to ~$85B from $80B after its convertible stock sale was reportedly oversubscribed.

The demand comes as Google races to scale AI compute capacity for what it called “unprecedented customer demand.”

$LMND expanded its autonomous car insurance product to $TSLA owners in Indiana offering up to 50% off miles driven using FSD Supervised.

The product uses Tesla driving data to price autonomous miles differently from manually driven miles.

$CRWD appointed former $NVDA agentic AI and cybersecurity AI leader Dr. Bartley Richardson as Chief AI Officer.

He will oversee Charlotte AI, AI Detection & Response and CrowdStrike’s broader push toward “Security AGI.”

$NVDA CEO Jensen Huang reportedly told investors in Taipei that AI ROI has been “completely reset” and is now “insanely profitable.”

It really does feel like the debate has shifted from whether AI spending has ROI to how fast companies can deploy enough compute to capture it.
I currently own a 3% position in $NVTS in my Growth Portfolio:

"Google ($GOOGL) is probably the best pure play bet on AI you can own."

Search. YouTube. Android. Advertising. Travel. The leverage across every platform is unlike anything else in the market right now.

The market keeps treating the Strait of Hormuz like a temporary inconvenience.

@biancoresearch thinks that's a massive mistake.

Global oil inventories are being drained to keep prices under control. If this disruption lasts longer than expected, inventories hit operational minimums and force demand destruction.

Translation: oil could go WAY higher than markets are pricing in.

Treating a structural problem like a temporary one is how you get blindsided.

Every single time.

FT @BitcoinJesusETH

The global AI boom has created a memory crunch, and a compelling new supplier is emerging to help meet it.

ChangXin Memory Technologies, China's largest dynamic, rapid-access memory (DRAM) maker, just filed to list on China's STAR Market, adding a credible fourth player to a global memory landscape dominated by @Samsung, @SKhynix, and @MicronTech.

With profits reportedly surging and technology advancing toward high-bandwidth memory capabilities, ChangXin could play a meaningful role in the AI supply chain for years to come.

ChangXin is not yet publicly traded, but the KraneShares SSE STAR Market 50 Index ETF (Ticker: $KSTR) could be a way for investors to gain exposure to this IPO when it lists.

Read our latest article: rebrand.ly/60f063

For KSTR's top ten holdings: rebrand.ly/1989d3
